findings.evidence: grep: new theorem at `LegendrePrimeDiscriminants.lean:72` has no attribute, while `LegendrePrimeDiscriminant.lean:82` marks `legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_one_iff` as `@[simp]` and `LegendreEvenPrimeDiscriminant.lean:159,166,173` marks the concrete even `eq_one_iff` lemmas as `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to this theorem, as in the existing pointwise odd/concrete-even Legendre APIs.
findings.issue: `legendreSym_primeDiscriminant_eq_one_iff` is the main characteristic rewrite for the new uniform prime-discriminant symbol, but it is not marked `@[simp]`.

findings.evidence: grep: `LegendrePrimeDiscriminants.lean:109` introduces this pointwise radicand `eq_one_iff` without an attribute; the surrounding public API uses simp for pointwise Legendre `eq_one_iff` normal forms (`LegendrePrimeDiscriminant.lean:82`, `LegendreEvenPrimeDiscriminant.lean:159`).
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` so `simp [hD, hq]` can normalize the splitting condition on `primeDiscriminantRadicand D` to the congruence/reciprocal criterion.
findings.issue: `legendreSym_primeDiscriminantRadicand_eq_one_iff` is the radicand-side characteristic rewrite consumed after the multiquadratic splitting law, but it is also exported without `@[simp]`.

summary: The exported characteristic API is missing the expected automation annotations for its main pointwise normal forms.
